caption:
Women’s basketball fans, this is your personal postseason. Introducing: The Women’s Sports Bar League™, hosted by 5wins.

For this year’s women’s NCAA basketball tournament, we’re calling for fans across the country to join our bracket challenge either individually or through our Women’s Sports Bar League™, capped off with watch parties at participating locations.

Scroll for prizes ➡️ and help your bar take home the trophy. Plus, we'll be giving out weekly prizes! 

Brackets open on March 16th and will be open through March 20th at 12 p.m. EST! 🤭🏀 but you can sign up to get a reminder email at the link in our bio.  Get hyped for the best time of the year!

caption:
For the second year in a row, all four 1-seeds are in the Final Four. And, the Final Four of UConn, South Carolina, UCLA and Texas are the same as last year.

We previewed both of tonight's matchups with our picks of who's heading to Sunday's national championship. More on [5wins.com] or the link in our bio.

#NCAAWBB

caption:
A new era of women's sports is being built.

The next generation of professional sports is redefining structure, power, and sustainability in real time. Take a look at a few of the biggest leagues that have emerged and how their atypical models both benefit athletes and provide new challenges for women's sports.

Read at the link in our bio or on [5wins.com].

B1G moves for women's college volleyball today 🏐🤝

The Big Ten Conference and Southeastern Conference have announced the Big Ten/SEC Volleyball Challenge Week, an annual series starting this September. The challenge will include matchups for all Big Ten and SEC teams, finishing with a doubleheader featuring Nebraska, Penn State, Kentucky and Missouri on Sept. 6 at Wrigley Field—home of the Chicago Cubs.

THIS WEEKEND: LSU and Oklahoma women's softball will face off in a three-game series. 🥎✨ And that means a matchup between Sydney Berzon and Paytn Monticelli—two players who effectively switched spots and transferred from one school to the other.

What really wins in March? 🎙️ 

In Episode 2 of Bracket Busters: A Coach’s Perspective, Niya Butts—former Division I women’s basketball head coach and current sports analyst—draws on her journey from NCAA National Champion at Tennessee to leading programs at Arizona and beyond. Her and Nyah discuss why guard play sets the tone, how rebounding and possessions decide games, and the leadership, grit, and urgency every tournament team needs. 

Now available to stream wherever you get your podcasts—link in bio!

caption:
Iowa State's Audi Crooks shared this morning that she will be entering the transfer portal and leaving Iowa State for her senior season of NCAA basketball. The announcement comes after the Cyclones' first round exit of the NCAA tournament in a season where Crooks averaged 25.8 points per game and 7.7 rebounds.

Crooks is now the 10th player from Iowa State to enter the transfer portal.

#ncaawbb #audicrooks

A historic season for Vanderbilt, AND there’s still more to come. 🤩

Sophomore guard Mikayla Blakes and Head Coach Shea Ralph have led the Commodores through an unprecedented winning season. Will their efforts be rewarded in the postseason?

caption:
🏀 Battle of the Brackets is back! 🏆

For the second year in a row we’re throwing down with 20+ women’s sports bars from across the country to see who balls the hardest. It's March Madness, baby, and it’s time to show out for Jolene Jolene! 🔥

Here’s how it works:
🏀  Join the Jolene Jolene pool (link in bio)
🏀  Fill out your bracket before noon on 3/20

What’s on the line?
🥇 Ultimate bragging rights + champion trophy + $200 VISA gift card + special prize from your bar
🥈 2nd place prize + swag package
🥉 3rd place prize + stickers

Think you’ve got the winning bracket? Good. Because I want that trophy back from @thesportsbrapdx!
